qa_pairs.question: How do you submit an Ansys Workbench job to a Slurm queue using a script configured for `StdEnv/2020`?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit an Ansys Workbench job, first prepare your Workbench project file (e.g., `YOURPROJECT.wbpj`). Then, use a Slurm script like `script-wbpj-2020.sh`, which loads `StdEnv/2020` and `ansys/2022R2` (or older Ansys module versions). This script specifies resources like time and memory, and uses the command `runwb2 -B -E "Update();Save(Overwrite=True)" -F YOURPROJECT.wbpj` to execute the project. For scaling tests, it's recommended to change `Save(Overwrite=True)` to `Save(Overwrite=False)` in the last line of the script to avoid overwriting the solution with each test run.

qa_pairs.question: How do you generate an input file for Ansys Mechanical APDL, and what are the basic commands for submitting CPU-based Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) and Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) jobs using Slurm scripts?
qa_pairs.answer: Ansys Mechanical APDL input files (e.g., `YOURAPDLFILE.inp`) can be generated from within your interactive Workbench Mechanical session by clicking `Solution -> Tools -> Write Input Files`. For CPU-based Shared Memory Parallel (SMP) jobs, a Slurm script would use the command `mapdl -smp -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp`, typically with `--nodes=1`. For Distributed Memory Parallel (DMP) jobs, the command would be `mapdl -dis -mpi intelmpi -b nolist -np $SLURM_NTASKS -dir outdir-$SLURM_JOBID -i YOURAPDLFILE.inp` (using Intel MPI, for example, on the Beluga cluster) or `mapdl -dis -mpi openmpi ...` (for other clusters with Open MPI). Both types of jobs typically load `StdEnv/2023` and `ansys/2024R1.03` (or `2023R2`).
